persons.arguments_against: Strongest counter-case: Dias is a very common Ceylonese surname (14 officials, 8 biographies in the corpus), so this stint could belong to a different F. R. Dias, or could even conflate two men — note the 1900 edition lists both 'F. R. Dias, District Judge' and 'Felix R. Dias, Crown Counsel', and the 1905-06 rows revert to 'Crown Counsel' although the bio has him as additional district judge from July 1900. Could the district-judge rows belong to a relative? This fails: (1) the stint prints the full forename 'Felix R. Dias', agreeing with FELIX REGINALD, not a bare initial; (2) 'Crown Counsel' is a specific senior law-office post, and the stint holds it from 1894, exactly matching 'crown counsel for the island, July, 1893'; (3) the bio itself bridges the two tracks — 'additnl. dist. judge, Colombo, July, 1900' explains the dual 1900 rows as a mid-year transition by one man, and 'Additional District Judge' in 1907-08 matches 'ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Apl., 1907'; (4) the District of Colombo and Midland Circuit covers Kandy, matching 'dist. judge, Kandy, Jan., 1908'. The 1905-06 Crown Counsel rows are best read as list lag or resumed counsel duties, not as a second Felix R. Dias — no second man of that forename appears anywhere in the rows.

evidence_cited.fact: crown counsel for the island, July, 1893

persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second 'Felix R. Dias' in the Ceylon establishment in this period (e.g. two contemporaneous Felix R. Dias rows in distinct posts in years other than the 1900 transition year), or a record showing the bio holder left the Colombo/Midland Circuit bench while the staff-list rows continue.

notes: The 1905-1906 'Crown Counsel' rows postdate the bio's July 1900 move to additional district judge; reviewer may wish to confirm whether he resumed Crown Counsel duties c.1905-06 or the staff list lagged. This affects the internal chronology only, not the identity of the stint holder.
